engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Dokker Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Dokker in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Dokker K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade from 90k miles, causing rough idle and misfires.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow, triggering fault codes and reduced power.
DPF blockage - short-journey driving causes soot accumulation requiring regeneration or replacement.

cards.altText: Renault Dokker H4M engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil seal wear - valve cover and sump gaskets leak after 70k miles on higher-mileage units.
Timing chain tensioner - rare but documented wear causing rattle on cold start after 100k miles.

cards.altText: Renault Dokker H5Ft engine - petrol 1.2L turbo replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - premature wear documented from 60k miles, risking catastrophic engine failure.
Excessive oil consumption - piston ring design flaw causes burning oil and blue exhaust smoke.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,600-£4,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable H5Ft units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Dokker Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Dokker across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 75hp diesel for Authentique/Expression; robust but basic, high fleet usage drives replacement demand.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow from 80k miles, triggering fault codes and limp mode.
Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade, causing rough idle and increased fuel consumption.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 612 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Most common 90hp K9K variant; fitted to Expression and Van models, dominant in UK commercial use.
commonFailures: DPF clogging - short-journey driving causes soot accumulation requiring forced regeneration or replacement.
Turbo actuator failure - vacuum-controlled wastegate sticks, causing boost loss and reduced power.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 636 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: 95hp facelift K9K with FAP/DPF; improved emissions compliance for Stepway and late-model Van.
compatibleVariants: Stepway 1.5 dCi 95, Van 1.5 dCi 95 Blue dCi
commonFailures: AdBlue system faults - NOx sensor or injector failures trigger emissions warnings and reduced performance.
EGR cooler leaks - internal coolant leakage causes white smoke and overheating on high-mileage units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 646 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Blue dCi Diesel
entries.history: Latest Euro 6 K9K with AdBlue; 95-110hp outputs for Stepway and commercial variants requiring ULEZ compliance.
compatibleVariants: Stepway 1.5 Blue dCi 95, Stepway 4x4 1.5 dCi 110, Van 1.5 Blue dCi 95
commonFailures: SCR catalyst degradation - AdBlue quality issues cause NOx conversion failure and MOT emissions test failure.
High-pressure fuel pump wear - internal component fatigue leads to hard starting and power loss after 100k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 872 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable 102hp naturally aspirated petrol; simple design with minimal electronics, popular for low-cost replacements.
commonFailures: Oil seal leaks - valve cover and sump gaskets deteriorate after 70k miles, causing external oil loss.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rare but documented rattle on cold start after 100k miles on neglected units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for H4M 737 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Updated 115hp H4M for facelift Stepway; minor internal revisions for improved refinement and emissions.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves - direct injection variants suffer reduced airflow and hesitation after 80k miles.
Thermostat housing cracks - plastic component failure causes coolant loss and overheating warnings.
entries.cta: Get quotes for H4M 738 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 85hp petrol for early Authentique; simple, low-output unit with minimal failure points but limited power.
commonFailures: No widely reported failure pattern specific to this model - general wear items apply.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K7M 812 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol (Van)
entries.history: Commercial-spec 102hp H4M tuned for Van duty; identical core to passenger variants but with commercial calibration.
commonFailures: Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear on high-mileage commercial units causes blue exhaust smoke.
entries.cta: Get quotes for H4M 812 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Downsized 115hp turbo petrol; high specific output but timing chain and oil consumption issues drive replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Expression 1.2 TCe 115, Stepway 1.2 TCe 115, Van 1.2 TCe 115
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch - premature wear from 60k miles risks catastrophic failure if not addressed preventatively.
Excessive oil consumption - piston ring design flaw causes burning oil and requires frequent top-ups after 70k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for H5Ft 402 Renault Dokker engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Late-model 1.3L turbo replacement for H5Ft; improved reliability but limited Dokker fitment before production ended.
commonFailures: No widely reported failure pattern specific to this model - general turbo petrol maintenance applies.
entries.cta: Get quotes for H5Ht Renault Dokker engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Renault Dokker Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Dokker's 1.5 dCi diesel and 1.6 SCe petrol engines are generally robust, but the 1.2 TCe turbo petrol has documented timing chain and oil consumption issues driving replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Renault Dokker 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Renault Dokker 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure - Why H5Ft Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Renault Dokker Expression, Stepway, Van 1.2 TCe 115 (2014-2021, H5Ft 402)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner design allows premature stretch; chain skips teeth causing valve timing loss and potential piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides and seals before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents catastrophic damage if done before 70k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, chain and piston rings; bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only sensible option once chain has skipped; avoids repeated repairs and downtime

problems.recommendation: On a Renault Dokker 1.2 TCe with timing chain rattle or fault codes, preventative replacement is cost-effective if mileage is under 70k. Once the chain has skipped or the engine has suffered damage, a rebuilt H5Ft unit with upgraded components offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H5Ft 402 engine prices for your Renault Dokker

problems.h4: Renault Dokker 1.5 dCi Injector Failure - Why K9K Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Renault Dokker Authentique, Expression, Van, Stepway 1.5 dCi (2012-2021, K9K 612/636/646)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure piezo injectors degrade internally; leakage causes poor atomisation, misfires and increased fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injector with coding; may require ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if only one injector failed and engine is otherwise healthy under 100k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with new injectors, upgraded seals and full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple injectors failed or engine has other wear; avoids repeated repairs

problems.recommendation: For a single injector fault on a low-mileage Dokker 1.5 dCi, replacement is economical. If the vehicle has exceeded 100k miles or shows multiple injector codes, a rebuilt K9K engine with new injectors fitted offers better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Dokker diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Renault Dokker 1.5 dCi DPF Clogging - Why Short Journeys Cause Blockages
problems.affectedModels: Renault Dokker Expression, Stepway, Van 1.5 dCi FAP/Blue dCi (2016-2021, K9K 646/872)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short trips prevent active DPF regeneration; soot accumulates beyond cleaning capacity, triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Professional off-vehicle cleaning or replacement with ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if blockage is caught early and driving patterns can be adjusted

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K with DPF
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with new or cleaned DPF, full emissions compliance and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if DPF is severely blocked or engine has other wear; ensures ULEZ compliance

problems.recommendation: If your Dokker 1.5 dCi is used primarily for short urban journeys, DPF issues are likely to recur. A rebuilt K9K engine with a new or professionally cleaned DPF, combined with adjusted driving habits, offers the most reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare K9K FAP Renault Dokker engine prices for ULEZ compliance

problems.group: Renault Dokker 1.6 SCe Oil Leak Repair
problems.h4: Renault Dokker 1.6 SCe Oil Leak Repair - Common Seal Failures on H4M Engines
problems.affectedModels: Renault Dokker Expression, Stepway, Van 1.6 SCe (2012-2021, H4M 737/738)
problems.rootCause: Valve cover gasket and sump seal materials harden with age; heat cycles cause leaks that worsen if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve cover gasket, sump seal and associated seals; clean oil residue
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage leaks on otherwise healthy engines under 90k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt H4M with all new seals, gaskets and updated components; 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if leaks are severe, oil loss is significant, or engine shows other wear signs

problems.recommendation: For minor external oil leaks on a low-mileage 1.6 SCe Dokker, seal replacement is cost-effective. If oil consumption is high or leaks are internal, a rebuilt H4M engine prevents recurring issues and provides warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt H4M Renault Dokker petrol engine availability

problems.h4: Renault Dokker Engine Won't Start - Diagnosis & Replacement Decision Guide
problems.affectedModels: All Renault Dokker variants (2012-2021, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Variable - often 80,000+ miles or after missed servicing
problems.rootCause: Multiple potential causes: fuel pump failure, immobiliser fault, crank sensor failure, or catastrophic mechanical damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full diagnostic scan, replace faulty sensor/pump/immobiliser component
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if fault is isolated and engine compression is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine matching original code; includes all ancillaries transfer and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if diagnosis reveals internal damage, low compression, or multiple failing components

problems.recommendation: Start with a professional diagnostic to isolate the fault. If the issue is a single component like a crank sensor or fuel pump, repair is economical. If compression is low or multiple systems have failed, a rebuilt engine offers better value and reliability.
problems.cta: Get instant Renault Dokker engine replacement quotes after diagnosis

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Dokker
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing chain worries on a 1.2 TCe, injector issues on a 1.5 dCi, or oil leaks on a 1.6 SCe, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Dokker specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Dokker Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Dokker and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault Dokker Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault Dokker in the UK is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Renault Dokker 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel is the backbone of the Renault Dokker range, fitted to Authentique, Expression, Van and Stepway variants from 2012-2021. This robust common-rail turbo diesel offers strong fuel economy and torque for commercial use, though EGR, injector and DPF issues on higher-mileage units drive replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: turbo actuator faults on pre-2018 units
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Dokker 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Dokker 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L naturally aspirated petrol (K7M/H4M) powered entry-level Authentique and mid-spec Expression/Stepway variants from 2012-2021. Simple, reliable design with minimal electronics makes these engines straightforward to replace, though oil seal leaks and rare timing chain tensioner wear occur on high-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: sump oil seal leaks after 70k miles
rare timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start after 100k miles
thermostat housing cracks on facelift models
items.cta: Compare Renault Dokker 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Dokker 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L TCe turbo petrol (H5Ft) was offered in Expression, Stepway and Van variants from 2014-2021 to provide downsized efficiency with 115hp output. However, documented timing chain stretch and excessive oil consumption issues from 60-70k miles make this engine a high-priority replacement candidate for affected owners.
commonFailurePoints: tensioner failure from 60k miles risking catastrophic damage
excessive oil consumption due to piston ring design flaw
items.cta: Compare Renault Dokker 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Dokker 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L TCe turbo petrol (H5Ht) appeared in very late-production Stepway variants (2020-2021) as a more reliable successor to the problematic 1.2 TCe. Limited UK fitment before Dokker production ended means replacement demand is currently low, but availability is growing as these units enter the secondary market.
commonFailurePoints: No widely reported failure pattern specific to Renault Dokker fitment; general turbo petrol maintenance applies
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Dokker 1.3L petrol engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Renault Dokker Engines by Fuel Type

items.title: Renault Dokker Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered most Renault Dokker variants from 2012-2021, dominating UK commercial and high-mileage private use with strong economy but documented EGR, injector and DPF issues driving replacement demand.
items.descriptor: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered most Renault Dokker variants from 2012-2021, dominating UK commercial and high-mileage private use with strong economy but documented EGR, injector and DPF issues driving replacement demand.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Dokker Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K 612 - entry 75hp for Authentique/Expression, basic but robust, high fleet usage
K9K 636 - most common 90hp variant for Expression/Van, dominant UK commercial fitment
K9K 646/872 - facelift 95-110hp Blue dCi with FAP/AdBlue for Stepway/Van, Euro 6 compliance
foundIn: Pre-facelift Dokker (2012-2016) - K9K 612/636, non-FAP on early models
Facelift Dokker (2016-2021) - K9K 646/872 with FAP/AdBlue for emissions compliance
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy (55-65 mpg real-world) ideal for commercial users
EGR valve and injector wear from 90k miles requiring attention on high-mileage units
DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles; regeneration or replacement often needed after 80k miles
High UK replacement demand on Expression and Van variants used for delivery/fleet work

items.title: Renault Dokker Petrol Engines
items.description: Naturally aspirated 1.6L petrol (K7M/H4M) and turbo 1.2/1.3L TCe units powered entry and mid-spec Dokker variants, offering simpler maintenance but with the 1.2 TCe suffering documented timing chain and oil consumption issues.
items.descriptor: Naturally aspirated 1.6L petrol (K7M/H4M) and turbo 1.2/1.3L TCe units powered entry and mid-spec Dokker variants, offering simpler maintenance but with the 1.2 TCe suffering documented timing chain and oil consumption issues.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Dokker Petrol Engine Families

H4M 737/738 - reliable 102-115hp for Expression/Stepway, oil seal leaks common after 70k miles
H5Ft 402 - 115hp turbo 1.2 TCe, high specific output but timing chain stretch risk from 60k miles
knownFor: Naturally aspirated 1.6L units offer simple, low-cost repairs and good reliability when maintained
1.2 TCe timing chain stretch documented from 60k miles; preventative replacement recommended
Oil consumption issues on high-mileage H4M and H5Ft units; regular checks essential
Lower replacement demand than diesel but growing for 1.2 TCe due to known failure patterns

items.title: Renault Dokker Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: No hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric variants of the Renault Dokker were officially sold in the UK market; all engines are conventional petrol or diesel.
items.descriptor: No hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric variants of the Renault Dokker were officially sold in the UK market; all engines are conventional petrol or diesel.

engineYears.h2: Renault Dokker Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Dokker's UK production timeline (2012-2021), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Renault Dokker launched with simple K9K diesel and K7M/H4M petrol engines, establishing the model's commercial and private appeal.
keyChanges: Renault Dokker launched in UK with Authentique and Expression trims
1.5 dCi K9K 612/636 diesel and 1.6 SCe K7M/H4M petrol engines introduced
Van variant added for commercial users with reinforced suspension
knownFor: Simple, robust engines with minimal electronics aid reliability and low-cost repairs
Non-FAP diesel variants avoid DPF concerns but may face ULEZ charges in cities
Early H4M petrol units show excellent longevity with basic maintenance
High commercial usage of Van variants drives replacement demand from 2018 onwards
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2015 Renault Dokker replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Dokker 1.5 dCi engine replacement • Renault Dokker Expression engine cost • Renault Dokker Van diesel supply and fit
enquiries: Renault Dokker Van diesel supply and fit

years.description: The 2016 facelift introduced updated K9K engines with FAP/DPF and the problematic 1.2 TCe turbo petrol, changing the replacement landscape.
keyChanges: Facelift styling and interior updates across Dokker range
Stepway crossover variant launched with raised suspension and styling
K9K 646 diesel with FAP/DPF introduced for emissions compliance
H5Ft 402 1.2 TCe turbo petrol added to Expression/Stepway/Van
knownFor: FAP/DPF-equipped diesels require active regeneration; short journeys cause blockages
1.2 TCe timing chain stretch emerges as major failure pattern from 60k miles
Stepway's crossover appeal increases private ownership and replacement enquiries
Van variants with FAP face commercial users needing ULEZ compliance solutions
years.ticker: Renault Dokker 1.2 TCe timing chain • Renault Dokker Stepway engine replacement • Renault Dokker FAP DPF clogging fix
enquiries: Renault Dokker FAP DPF clogging fix

years.description: Final production years focused on Euro 6 Blue dCi compliance with AdBlue, while 1.2 TCe issues drove increased replacement demand.
keyChanges: K9K 872 Blue dCi with AdBlue introduced for full Euro 6 compliance
Stepway 4x4 variant offered with 110hp K9K and all-wheel drive
H5Ht 1.3 TCe appeared in very limited late-production Stepway units
Renault Dokker production ended in 2021 across all variants
knownFor: Blue dCi AdBlue systems require fluid top-ups; sensor faults trigger warnings
Final-year 1.2 TCe units still suffer timing chain issues; rebuilt engines with upgrades essential
Stepway 4x4 rarity drives specialist replacement demand and higher pricing
End of production increases demand for rebuilt units as used supply diminishes
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2021 Renault Dokker replacement engines

faq.h2: Renault Dokker Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Dokker engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Renault Dokker engine prices range from £1,200 for a basic K9K diesel to £4,200 for a 1.2 TCe turbo petrol, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on garage location and engine complexity.
keyPoints: K9K 1.5 dCi diesel rebuilt: £1,200-£3,800 supply only
H4M 1.6 SCe petrol rebuilt: £1,400-£3,200 supply only
H5Ft 1.2 TCe turbo petrol rebuilt: £1,600-£4,200 supply only
Supply & fit service typically adds £400-£900 to engine price
Prices vary by engine code, mileage verification and supplier location
items.cta: Compare Renault Dokker engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most common Renault Dokker engine problem?
items.answer: The 1.2 TCe turbo petrol's timing chain stretch is the most documented failure, risking catastrophic damage from 60k miles. On diesel 1.5 dCi units, injector wear and DPF clogging drive most replacement enquiries.
keyPoints: H5Ft 1.2 TCe: timing chain stretch from 60k miles, oil consumption issues
K9K 1.5 dCi: injector wear from 90k miles, EGR/DPF faults on high mileage
H4M 1.6 SCe: oil seal leaks after 70k miles, generally reliable otherwise
Always verify engine code before ordering replacement parts or engines
Preventative maintenance can avoid costly failures on all engine types
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific Renault Dokker engine code

items.question: Is a reconditioned Renault Dokker engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, when sourced from vetted UK specialists. Reconditioned Renault Dokker engines undergo inspection, cleaning and replacement of worn seals/gaskets, typically with a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty for peace of mind.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines replace seals, gaskets and wear items after thorough inspection
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on EngineMarket network units
Ideal for K9K diesel and H4M petrol engines with good block condition
Always verify supplier credentials and warranty terms before committing
Rebuilt option available for maximum longevity with upgraded internal components
items.cta: Check reconditioned Renault Dokker engine availability

items.question: How do I find my Renault Dokker engine code?
items.answer: Your Renault Dokker engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox) and listed on the VIN plate under the bonnet or in your V5C logbook under section D.2.
keyPoints: Check engine block stamp near gearbox for code like K9K 636 or H4M 737
VIN plate under bonnet or V5C logbook section D.2 shows engine code
Enter registration on EngineMarket for instant DVLA-verified engine identification
Common codes: K9K (diesel), H4M/K7M (1.6L petrol), H5Ft (1.2 TCe)
Correct code ensures compatibility and avoids costly ordering mistakes
items.cta: Use our free Renault Dokker engine code lookup tool

items.question: Can I replace my Renault Dokker 1.2 TCe with a 1.5 dCi diesel?
items.answer: Technically possible but not recommended. Swapping petrol to diesel in a Renault Dokker requires ECU reprogramming, wiring harness changes, emissions system modifications and may fail MOT or invalidate insurance.
keyPoints: Engine swap requires ECU coding, wiring changes and emissions system adaptation
May fail MOT emissions test or invalidate vehicle insurance coverage
Legal paperwork and DVLA notification required for fuel type changes
Rebuilt 1.2 TCe with timing chain upgrades is safer, legal and warranty-backed
Always consult a specialist before attempting cross-fuel engine swaps
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt H5Ft 1.2 TCe Renault Dokker engines

items.question: Does Renault Dokker engine replacement include a warranty?
items.answer: Yes. Every rebuilt or reconditioned Renault Dokker engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Terms vary by supplier; always confirm exact coverage before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on network engines
Warranty covers internal components; ancillaries may have separate terms
Keep service records and use correct oil specs to maintain warranty validity
Supply & fit installations often include labour warranty from fitting garage
Always get warranty terms in writing before confirming your order
items.cta: Compare Renault Dokker engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How long does Renault Dokker engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 2-4 days from order to collection: 24-48 hours for engine dispatch plus 1 day for professional fitting, testing and handover at your local garage.
keyPoints: Engine dispatch: 24-48 hours from order confirmation for in-stock units
Professional fitting: typically 1 working day at vetted UK garage
Total turnaround: 2-4 days depending on engine availability and garage schedule
Mobile fitting available in some areas for added convenience
Commercial Van users can request priority scheduling to minimise downtime
items.cta: Book Renault Dokker engine supply & fit at a local garage

items.question: Are used Renault Dokker engines worth buying?
items.answer: Used engines offer the lowest upfront cost but carry variable condition risk. For budget repairs on older Authentique models they can be sensible; for Expression/Stepway/Van, reconditioned units with warranty offer better long-term value.
keyPoints: Used engines: £800-£2,400 but condition and mileage vary significantly
Always request compression test results and service history before purchase
Typical warranty only 3-6 months vs 12 months on reconditioned units
Reconditioned option adds £400-£800 but includes new seals and warranty
Best for low-budget repairs on pre-2016 Authentique with simple K7M engines
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Renault Dokker engine options

items.question: Will a replacement Renault Dokker engine be ULEZ compliant?
items.answer: Compliance depends on the original vehicle's emissions standard, not the replacement engine. A Euro 6 K9K 872 Blue dCi replacement maintains ULEZ compliance; older Euro 5 units do not, regardless of engine condition.
keyPoints: ULEZ compliance is based on vehicle's original Euro rating, not replacement engine
Euro 6 K9K 872 Blue dCi engines maintain compliance for eligible vehicles
Euro 5 K9K 636/646 replacements do not make non-compliant vehicles ULEZ-exempt
Petrol replacements (H4M/H5Ft) are generally ULEZ-compliant if vehicle was originally petrol
Check your vehicle's Euro rating on the TfL website before ordering
items.cta: Enquire about Euro 6 Renault Dokker engine availability

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Renault Dokker engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines receive cleaning, inspection and replacement of seals/gaskets. Rebuilt engines undergo full machining, new internal components and upgrades targeting known failures like timing chains, offering maximum longevity.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: inspection, cleaning, new seals/gaskets; ideal for good-condition blocks
Rebuilt: full machining, new pistons/rings, upgraded components for known failure points
Rebuilt recommended for H5Ft 1.2 TCe needing timing chain and piston ring upgrades
Both include 12-month warranty; rebuilt typically £200-£600 more than reconditioned
Choose rebuilt for high-mileage vehicles or maximum long-term reliability
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Renault Dokker engine prices
